rulings.Case Title: LESTER ROYAL et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 10, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Plaintiffs¿ motion for preference is granted per C.C.P. 36(a). Trial is September 4, 2012 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day for hearing summary motions is August 23. The court will ask whether plaintiffs will stipulate that summary motions may be heard on 15 days notice without an additional two days for e-service. If plaintiffs agree, the last day to hear summary motions remains August 23, with oppositions due five court days before the hearing and replies due two court days before the hearing. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on all defendants no later than August 6, 2012. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30pm on August 23, 2012. Fact discovery cut-off date is August 17, 2012. Expert discovery cut-off date is August 31, 2012. No later than June 1, 2012, plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs¿ claims. No later than June 1, 2012, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant¿s affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. The parties to meet and confer regarding any other scheduling or other issue which any of them want to have included in the preference order. Further case management conference on August 8, 2012 at 1:30pm. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 and the date of this conference will be provided to the parties by the court at a later date. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am in Department 602, Hon. Suzanne R. Bolanos presiding. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/SRB)
